perturbation.original_text: 3.2.1. You must pay us a royalty fee ("Royalty") equal to six percent (6%) of your Gross Revenues. The Royalty is in consideration of your right to use the Proprietary Marks and the System in accordance with this Agreement, and not in exchange for any specific services we render. If by reason of state or other law, we are prohibited from receiving a percentage of certain components of Gross Revenues (including alcoholic-beverage sales), you must pay us an equivalent amount by increasing the Royalty percentage applied to Gross Revenues exclusive of the prohibited components.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.2.1. You must pay us a royalty fee ("Royalty") equal to six percent (6%) of your Gross Revenues. The Royalty is in consideration of your right to use the Proprietary Marks and the System in accordance with this Agreement, and also in partial exchange for the ongoing support and marketing services that we provide to you. If by reason of state or other law, we are prohibited from receiving a percentage of certain components of Gross Revenues (including alcoholic-beverage sales), you are not required to pay an equivalent amount.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces ambiguity by stating the royalty is both 'not in exchange for any specific services' and 'also in partial exchange for ongoing support and marketing services,' creating an in-text contradiction. It also changes the clause for the Gross Revenues payment. This may lead to disputes about the franchisor's obligations and the basis for the royalty fee. Contradiction is in section 3.2.1 where the payment is being reffered in two different contradictory ways. This may create confusion on the meaning of the payment.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.2.1. You must pay us a royalty fee ("Royalty") equal to six percent (6%) of your Gross Revenues. The Royalty is in consideration of your right to use the Proprietary Marks and the System in accordance with this Agreement, and also in partial exchange for the ongoing support and marketing services that we provide to you. If by reason of state or other law, we are prohibited from receiving a percentage of certain components of Gross Revenues (including alcoholic-beverage sales), you are not required to pay an equivalent amount.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction within section 3.2.1. The original text states that the royalty fee is 'not in exchange for any specific services,' while the changed text states it is 'also in partial exchange for ongoing support and marketing services.' This creates ambiguity about the purpose of the royalty and violates the definition of Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction, as the terms are defined inconsistently within the same clause.

perturbation.original_text: 4.5 Opening Deadline. You must begin operating the Franchised Business by the opening deadline specified in Appendix A. The date you actually open the Franchised Business is the "Opening Date."
perturbation.changed_text: 4.5 Opening Deadline. You must begin operating the Franchised Business by the opening deadline specified in Appendix A, unless unforeseen circumstances arise. The date you actually open the Franchised Business is the "Projected Opening Date."
perturbation.explanation: By adding 'unless unforeseen circumstances arise,' the obligation to meet the opening deadline becomes less binding, contradicting the earlier strict requirement. Renaming the actual opening day to the Projected Opening Date adds a certain ambivalence regarding this term. This creates an in-text contradiction if other sections penalize the franchisee for failing to meet the opening deadline. Contradiction is in section 4.5 where the 'Opening Deadline' is not as binding as may be interpreted from reading the changed text, unlike the original.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.3 Location Development and Preparation. You must construct, furnish, and open the Franchised Business as required by this Agreement and must open the Franchised Business not later than six (6) months after securing the necessary authorization and approval for permits and/or certificates. Time is of the essence.
perturbation.justification: The original text in 4.5 creates a strict 'Opening Deadline.' The changed text introduces an exception ('unless unforeseen circumstances arise') and renames the 'Opening Date' to 'Projected Opening Date,' creating ambiguity and undermining the strict obligation. This directly contradicts 4.3, which states 'Time is of the essence' and requires opening within six months, implying no exceptions. The change in 4.5 weakens the binding nature of the deadline, creating an in-text contradiction with the more rigid requirement in 4.3. This falls under Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 10.2 Data. You agree that all data that you collect from customers and potential customers in connection with the Franchised Business ("Customer Data") is deemed to be owned exclusively by us, and you also agree to provide the Customer Data to us at any time that we request as you to do so. You have the right to use Customer Data while this Agreement or a Successor Franchise Agreement is in effect, but only in connection with operating the Franchised Business and only in accordance with the policies that we establish from time to time. You may not sell, transfer, or use Customer Data for any purpose other than operating the Franchised Business and marketing "Pizza Fusion" products and services. However, if you Transfer the Franchised Business (as provided in Section 14.2 [No Transfer without Our Prior Written Consent] below), as part of the Transfer, you may Transfer use of the Customer Data to the buyer for value.
perturbation.changed_text: 10.2 Data. You agree that all data that you collect from customers and potential customers in connection with the Franchised Business ("Customer Data") is deemed to be owned exclusively by you, and you also agree to provide the Customer Data to us at any time that we request as you to do so. You have the right to use Customer Data while this Agreement or a Successor Franchise Agreement is in effect, but only in connection with operating the Franchised Business and only in accordance with the policies that we establish from time to time. You may not sell, transfer, or use Customer Data for any purpose other than operating the Franchised Business and marketing "Pizza Fusion" products and services. However, if you Transfer the Franchised Business (as provided in Section 14.2 [No Transfer without Our Prior Written Consent] below), as part of the Transfer, you must transfer the Customer Data to the buyer without any additional compensation.
perturbation.explanation: By changing ownership of data in this section, this introduces ambiguity by stating that customer data is owned by the the franchisee and also, by the franchisor; creating an in-text contradiction. Also, by changing the Customer Data clause after transfer, this makes it unclear the monetary terms. This may lead to disputes about the data ownership and the compensation for that data after transfer. Contradiction is in section 10.2 where the ownership and compensation data are being reffered in two different contradictory ways. This may create confusion on who is the legitimate owner of such data.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10.2 Data. You agree that all data that you collect from customers and potential customers in connection with the Franchised Business ("Customer Data") is deemed to be owned exclusively by you, and you also agree to provide the Customer Data to us at any time that we request as you to do so. You have the right to use Customer Data while this Agreement or a Successor Franchise Agreement is in effect, but only in connection with operating the Franchised Business and only in accordance with the policies that we establish from time to time. You may not sell, transfer, or use Customer Data for any purpose other than operating the Franchised Business and marketing "Pizza Fusion" products and services. However, if you Transfer the Franchised Business (as provided in Section 14.2 [No Transfer without Our Prior Written Consent] below), as part of the Transfer, you must transfer the Customer Data to the buyer without any additional compensation.
perturbation.justification: The modified text creates a strong in-text contradiction within Section 10.2 concerning the ownership and compensation of Customer Data upon the Franchised Business transfer. Originally, the franchisee could transfer the Customer Data for value. The changed text mandates that the franchisee must transfer the Customer Data 'without any additional compensation.' This directly contradicts the earlier part of Section 10.2, which also states that the data is owned exclusively by the franchisee, while simultaneously stating it's owned by the franchisor. This shift creates ambiguity and conflicts, thus causing a strong in-text contradiction.
